intro: Connect a data source, then go AI designs → review & refine → save. Once saved it appears in your datasets. Structure analysis runs automatically in the background, so press "View structure" only when you want to check it.

restore.note: Restored your previous work (re-select the source only if you need to re-run).

step.guard: Generate a schema first in "AI designs".

source.label: Data source
source.note: Any structured source planned (currently CSV / JSON / documents)
source.footSelected: {{n}} file(s) selected — the same source is used in every step
source.footEmpty: The source you choose here is shared across every step

design.hint: The AI proposes a draft of the data's blueprint (vocabulary) and ingestion rules. It uses the model selected in Settings.
design.hintsLegend: Hints (optional — checking the ones that apply improves accuracy)
design.domainFreePlaceholder: e.g. Seebeck = thermopower. Figures were read with WebPlotDigitizer.
design.inspectDetails: Show the structure analysis this proposal used

review.hint: Review the proposal; comment on what you want changed and the AI regenerates it (optional).
review.commentPlaceholder: e.g. Make the Sample IRI a composite key of (SID, sample_id). Update the ingester and design rationale to match.

save.hint: Split the schema into 4 artifacts, validate the 8 traps, and save to your datasets.

job.elapsed: {{mm}}m {{ss}}s elapsed · usually 1–6 min · connection is kept alive
job.elapsedStatus: {{mm}}m {{ss}}s elapsed · usually 1–6 min · connection is kept alive · status: {{status}}

materialize.summaryOk: No blocking failures (exit 0 = OK to save)

trap.T1: IRI uniqueness (is the composite key globally unique?)
trap.T2: BOM (does the ingester open with utf-8-sig?)
trap.T5: Mermaid colon avoidance (no : in diagram labels)

ingest.heading: Import data (human gate)
ingest.noRml: This design has no declarative RML mapping, so it cannot be ingested. Once propose emits §RML (the declarative mapping), you can safely ingest from here.
ingest.note: When you approve, Morph-KGC runs this declarative RML (no generated code runs — only the verified Tier 0 functions) and ingests the result into an isolated draft graph. It does not touch Ask's default citation surface (canonical).
ingest.doneCount: ✓ Ingested into the draft graph ({{n}} triples).
ingest.notSaved: This design has not been saved yet (no dataset found).
ingest.needCsv: Ingestion needs the CSV used in the design. Re-select the CSV in the data source panel (re-selection is required after a reload).

hints.compositeKey: ID columns may repeat across files/papers (a composite key may be needed)
hints.units: Normalize units and quantities (unify synonyms, etc.)
hints.jsonColumns: Some columns contain JSON (arrays or objects) inside cells
